data.title: ANZ Research Forecasts the Australian Central Bank to Cut Rates by 25 Basis Points in February
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of Australia is expected to cut the policy rate by 25 basis points at its February monetary policy meeting, with trimmed mean inflation forecast to decline to 0.5% quarter-over-quarter and 3.2% year-over-year, ANZ Research said in a note on Friday.

The forecast annual trimmed mean inflation is below the central bank's forecast of 3.4% year-over-year, while the six-month annualized rate for trimmed mean inflation is forecast to fall to 2.6%, around the middle of the Reserve Bank's target band of 2% to 3%.

Services inflation is estimated to drop to 4.1% year-over-year, the lowest since the third quarter of 2022.

It expects another rate cut in August, taking the cash rate to 3.85%.

However, the central bank could hold the policy rate, if concerns that the persistent tightness in the labor market and expected recovery in household spending growth still pose upside risks to inflation carry more weight, the note warned.

data.title: Waste management services on Deepsea Bollsta
data.description:
Soiltech ASA (OSE: STECH)Sandnes, Norway 10 January 2025

Soiltech ASA has been informed of the intention to award Soiltech an agreementfor the delivery of Drilling waste management services on the harshenvironmentdrilling rig Deepsea Bollsta. The contract is expected to commence in thesecondhalf of 2025. This is estimated to be a large contract for Soiltech¹.

Deepsea Bollsta will be working for Equinor in Norway for an estimated firmduration of two years, plus five one-year optional periods.

Soiltech will provide offshore cuttings handling services and processing ofallliquid waste fluid on the rig.

"We are thankful for being selected for this contract. By combining thecuttingshandling services with the liquid waste processing on the rig, we can providethe most efficient and cost-effective solution to the client", says ErikBjøndal-Røvde, Vice president operations in Soiltech.

About SoiltechSoiltech is an innovative technology company specializing in the treatment,recycling and sustainable handling of contaminated water and solid waste onsite. Our technologies enable cost savings and lower CO2 emissions throughwastereduction, waste recovery and reuse. Soiltech operates world-wide and is headquartered in Norway. This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to Section5-12 the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

____________1. Contract sizesA sizable contract has an estimated value of MNOK 5 - 10 over 12 months A substantial contract has an estimated value of MNOK 10 - 20 over 12 monthsA large contract has an estimated value above MNOK 20 over 12 months

data.title: Shandong Daye Moving to Full Ownership of Daye New Materials
data.description:
Wire and cord manufacturer Shandong Daye is planning to acquire a 19.4% stake in subsidiary Daye New Materials for 430.6 million yuan.

The stake will be acquired from Weifang Urban Construction Investment, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shandong Daye will fully own Daye New Materials following the stake acquisition.

data.title: Xizi Clean Energy Equipment Manufacturing Gets 6.16 Billion Yuan Worth of Orders in 2024; Shares Up 10%
data.description:
Xizi Clean Energy Equipment Manufacturing received 6.16 billion yuan worth of orders in 2024, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Among these are new orders worth 1.27 billion yuan and cumulative new orders of 5.85 billion yuan for the year, the Chinese waste heat recovery boiler manufacturer said.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Mount Gibson Iron Buys Back 186,170 Shares
data.description: Mount Gibson Iron repurchased 186,170 ordinary fully-paid shares for a total of about AU$55,610 in an on-market buyback, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
U.K. Pound, Bonds Slide; U.S. Stock Market Closed

Volatility in U.K. asset prices evokes memories of the 2022 market meltdown.

China's Central Bank Suspends Purchase of Treasury Bonds in Open Markets

The move comes as long-term yields hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

Investors are on edge as Friday's jobs report could make or break the stock-market rally

Rising Treasury yields are ratcheting up the anxiety for equity investors. Thursday's stock-market closure offered only a brief reprieve from the mounting tension.

The World's Bond Markets Are Uneasy. It's the U.S.'s Fault.

The yield on 10-year U.K. government debt rose to the highest since 2008, the latest casualty of a global rout spurred by rising yields on Treasury notes.

The Stock Market Is Looking for Its Next Boost. Earnings Won't Provide.

Companies across the board are less likely to surpass expectations, while stocks have already taken the past few years to reflect growing earnings and a growing economy.

L.A. wildfires have caused more than $135 billion in economic losses - and counting

Fires across Southern California are already one of the most damaging in the states history.

Deficit Fears Are Driving Up Bond Yields. Stocks Could Take a Hit.

Rising Treasury yields are a risk for the stock market, strategists say.

U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Since July

U.S. mortgage rates are back at their highest level since last summer, frustrating home buyers waiting for a break from some of the steepest borrowing costs in more than a decade.

Why two Fed officials doubt the need for many more rate cuts

One official warned that "progress on inflation could continue to stall."

Bank of England Monitoring Gilt Market, But Sees Moves as Orderly, Says Breeden

"So far the moves have been orderly," Breeden said after a speech in Scotland. "So far so good."

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
TikTok Is Heading to the Supreme Court. How Meta and Snap Could Benefit from a Ban.

The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments from TikTok on Friday.

The Pacific Palisades Inferno Won't Engulf the Insurance Industry

The insurance industry has been thriving as climate change increases the demand for coverage.

Fox Must Face Smartmatic's Defamation Lawsuit, Appeals Court Rules

The voting-machine company seeks billions in damages.

BlackRock Withdraws From Climate Coalition, Backpedaling Again on ESG

The world's largest asset manager is latest Wall Street firm to quit climate groups.

Elon Musk's xAI Launches Stand-Alone Grok App

The AI startup launched its first stand-alone consumer app, as the company tries to catch up with more established players such as OpenAI and Google in the generative AI race.

Wells Fargo Taps JPMorgan Executive to Lead Its Credit-Card Business

Growth in credit cards has been a focus for CEO Charlie Scharf.

Delta Air Lines' Earnings Can Make or Break the Sector's Rally. 1 Metric Is Key.

Airline stocks have been flying recently, which makes earnings season tricky to navigate for investors.

Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo to Retire as Airline Embarks on Turnaround

Romo is retiring after more than three decades with the carrier as it works on a turnaround after lagging behind some of its peers in recent years.

DHL Buys Into the Growing Retail Returns Business

The acquisition of a returns services provider comes as retailers are grappling with the higher volumes that shoppers are sending back.

KKR, PSP Investments Partner to Buy Stake in Two American Electric Power Companies

KKR is partnering with the Public Sector Pension Investment Board to buy a 19.9% interest in two units of American Electric Power for $2.82 billion.

data.title: Yash Highvoltage Appoints UK Distributor for Select Products
data.description:
Yash Highvoltage has appointed Electrolink Power Solutions as its distributor for the United Kingdom and Ireland, excluding the Republic of Ireland, according to a Thursday filing with the BSE.

The distributor will promote and market products including LV high current bushings up to 25,000 amps and OIP condenser bushings up to 245 kV.

The agreement, effective Thursday, is valid for an initial 12 months and may be extended by mutual consent, Yash Highvoltage said in the filing.

data.title: Buy Tata Consultancy Services; target of Rs 4810: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Tata Consultancy Services

Revenue reported at USD 7.5 bn, flat QoQ in CC (down 1.7% QoQ in USD) vs our & cons. est. of 0.4% QoQ CC growth. The growth was led by regional market & India regions, primarily attributed to the continued ramp up of BSNL deal, although the incremental growth from the deal has come off substantially in 3Q and it would decelerate further in 4Q. EBIT margin increased by 40 bps QoQ to 24.5% largely in-line with cons. est, & below PLe of 24.8%, attributed to the tailwinds of operational efficiencies, improved utilization & pyramid optimization. Deal TCV was encouraging with strong wins reported in BFSI, Retail & North America leading to total TCV of USD 10.2 bn, up 18.6% QoQ & 25.9% YoY, which translates to BTB of 1.3x (TTM).

Outlook

We estimate USD revenue/earnings CAGR of 7.6%/13.1% over FY25-FY27E. The stock is currently trading at 23.3x FY27E, we are assigning P/E of 28x to FY27E with a target price of INR 4,810. We maintain “BUY” rating.

For all recommendations report, click here

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

Tata Consultancy Services - 10012025 - prabhu

data.title: Sensex Rises But Points to 3rd Successive Weekly Losses
data.description:
The stock market in India rose 101 points, or 0.1%, to 77,730 in the Friday morning session, halting losses from the previous two sessions, mainly boosted by gains in the tech sector.

The Nifty IT surged 2.5%, with TCS leading the gains.

Traders remained focused on the Q3 earnings reports, after TCS posted its Q3 consolidated net profit, in line with expectations.

The Nifty 50 rose by 0.2% to above 23,550, with notable gains from TCS (up 4.7%), which registered a 12% growth in its Q3 consolidated net profit; LTIMindtree (4.2%), Tech Mahindra (3.2%), Wipro (2.8%), and Persistent (2.1%).

By contrast, Tata Elxsi plunged 6.4% after Q3 results missed expectations, while IREDA dropped 2.3%.

Still, the BSE Sensex is heading for its third straight weekly decline, down nearly 2% so far, pressured by worries over disappointing Q3 earnings results, slow GDP growth, high Treasury yields, and uncertainty over additional tariffs under the Trump administration.

data.title: Finnish Current Account Surplus Narrows Sharply
data.description:
Finland's current account deficit narrowed sharply to EUR 324 million in November 2024 from EUR 1,451 million in the corresponding month of the previous year.

Goods account surplus went down significantly to EUR 654 million in November 2024 from EUR 2,395 million a year earlier.

Meanwhile, services account shortfall decreased to EUR 360 from EUR 809 million in November 2023.

Additionally, primary income surplus increased to EUR 233 million from EUR 75 million, while secondary income deficit narrowed slightly to EUR 203 million from EUR 209 million.

data.title: Napier Port Says Dec-Qtr Trade Volumes Increased 4.1% For Containerised Cargo
data.description:
Napier Port Holdings Ltd :

  • DEC QUARTER TRADE VOLUMES INCREASED 4.1% FOR CONTAINERISED CARGO

data.title: Japan's First 20 Days of December 2024 Show 3.8% Rise in Exports
data.description:
Japan's exports in the first 20 days of December 2024 totaled 6.581 trillion yen, a 3.8% increase from the same period last year, provisional data from the Ministry of Finance showed Friday.

Imports rose 5.6% to 7.328 trillion yen, resulting in a trade deficit of 746.69 billion yen, compared with a deficit of 601.37 billion yen in December 2023.

data.title: Indonesia Retail Sales to Continue to Increase in December 2024
data.description:
Retail sales in Indonesia are expected to continue to grow in December 2024, according to a Bank Indonesia survey released Thursday.

The real sales index is expected to rise by 1% year on year in the last monht of 2024 to reach to a level of 220.3, driven by increases seen in the spare parts and accessories, as well as the food, beverages, and tobacco subgroup.

Monthly, meanwhile, the real sales index is expected to rise by 5.1%, rebounding from a 0.4% contraction a month earlier, the central bank said.

data.title: Zhejiang Dehong Automotive Electronic & Electrical Increases Inventory of Asset Transfer Plan
data.description:
Zhejiang Dehong Automotive Electronic & Electrical revised its asset transfer plan to its wholly-owned subsidiary, adding additional inventory and equipment while excluding certain stock items, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Initially approved in December, the updated plan increases the total assets transferred to about 592.4 million yuan, with liabilities of 232.8 million yuan.

The internal restructuring aims to streamline resources and promote long-term growth. The revised plan has been approved by the board and supervisors but awaits shareholder approval.

data.title: Finnish Industrial Output Rises for 2nd Month
data.description:
Industrial production in Finland advanced by 3.2% year-on-year in November 2024, accelerating from a 0.6% rise in the previous month.

This marked the second consecutive month of growth in industrial activity, as output increased at a faster pace for both mining & quarrying (6.6% vs 4.4% in October) and manufacturing (3.9% vs 1%).

Meanwhile, production declined further for electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply (-2.6% vs -1.2%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial production grew by 1.4%, following a downwardly revised 0.4% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: NN Group: Contribution of NN Hayat to Group's Operating Result Is Limited
NN Group: Sale of Turkish Operations to Zurich Turkiye Announced Sept 25
NN Group: Sale Has a Negligible Impact on Group's Solvency II Ratio
Holcim's Board Proposes New Chairman
data.description:
Swiss building materials company Holcim's board proposed Kim Fausing as its new chairman.

Shareholders will vote on Fausing's appointment at the company's annual general meeting on May 14, according to a Friday release.

Fausing, currently president and chief executive officer at Danfoss Group, will succeed Jan Jenisch, who was appointed as the designated chairman and CEO of Holcim's planned North American company.

data.title: Australian Shares End Week Lower; Brookfield Purportedly Mulls Joining Bidding War for Insignia Financial
data.description:
Australian shares closed the week lower, tracking Asian markets, as investors await the US non-farm payrolls report later during the day that will shape the expectations for interest rate cuts.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index fell 0.4% or 35.5 points to close at 8,289.7.

On Thursday, Fed officials signaled that the central bank will hold the current interest rates of 4.35% for a longer period and that ongoing inflation risks warranted a cautious, gradual pace of rate cuts.

Analysts expect unemployment to hold at 4.2%, adding that "anything upbeat" may support the US Federal Reserve's case for fewer rate cuts within the year, Reuters had earlier reported.

In domestic news, Australia's household spending growth eased to 0.4% in November 2024 from 0.9% in the prior month, data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics showed.

Despite missing analysts' consensus, growth in Australia's retail sales is not as weak as it seems and is unlikely to impact the central bank's February decision, ANZ Research said.

In company news, Brookfield is mulling a takeover bid for Insignia Financial , The Australian reported. However, the asset manager declined to comment on the matter.

In a separate bourse filing, Insignia Financial said it has yet to receive a proposal from Brookfield. Its shares rose 2% on market close.

The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) alleges that Regional Express Holdings knew the financial health of the company was deteriorating after making a profit projection months earlier, The Australian reported based on court records.

Lastly, The Star Entertainment Group agreed to sell a luxury yacht to Platinum Capital Partners after allegedly reneging on the deal, settling a legal dispute involving the already struggling casino operator, the Australian Financial Review reported. Shares plunged 15% at market close, earlier hitting a 52-week low.

data.title: Japan's International Reserves Drop to $1.231 Trillion in December
data.description:
Japan's international reserves totaled $1.231 trillion at the end of December 2024, down $8.28 billion from November, data from the Ministry of Finance showed Friday.

The reserves included $1.077 trillion in foreign currency, $10.2 billion in IMF reserve position, $57.2 billion in special drawing rights (SDRs), and $71.01 billion in gold.

Other foreign currency assets were $38.76 billion, primarily loans to the Japan Bank for International Cooperation.

Short-term liabilities included a net short position in forward and futures contracts of $20 billion and contingent liabilities of $9.9 billion.

data.title: TSMC's fourth-quarter revenue handily beats market forecast on AI demand
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSMC Q4 revenue T$868.42 bln according to Reuters calculations
  • Revenue beats market forecast
  • TSMC has benefited from surge towards AI
  • Company to report Q4 earnings on Jan 16

TSMC , the world's largest contract chipmaker, reported on Friday fourth-quarter revenue that easily beat market forecasts and hit its own expectations as it reaped the benefit of artificial intelligence demand.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, whose customers include Apple and Nvidia , has been at the forefront of the march towards AI that has helped it weather the tapering off of pandemic-led demand of chips used in consumer electronics like tablets.

Revenue in the October-December period of 2024 came in at T$868.42 billion ($26.36 billion), according to Reuters calculations, compared with an LSEG SmartEstimate of T$853.57 billion ($25.90 billion) drawn from 23 analysts.

That represents growth of 34.4% year-on-year, and compares with revenue of $19.62 billion in the year-ago period.

It is not a direct comparison as TSMC provides monthly revenue data only in Taiwan dollars, but gives quarterly revenue figures and its outlook on its quarterly earnings calls both in U.S. dollars.

On its most recent earnings call in October, TSMC forecast fourth-quarter revenue in a range of between $26.1 billion to $26.9 billion.

For December alone, TSMC reported revenue jumped 57.8% year-on-year to T$278.16 billion.

The company did not provide details in its brief revenue statement.

Other Taiwan companies have also reported strong fourth-quarter revenue due to the AI boom. On Sunday, Foxconn , the world's largest contract electronics maker and which makes AI servers for Nvidia, beat expectations to post its highest-ever revenue for the final three months of 2024.

TSMC will report full fourth-quarter earnings on Jan. 16, when it will update its outlook for the current quarter and full year.

The company's Taipei listed stock leapt 81% last year, compared with a 28.5% gain for the broader market TWSE:TAIEX.

It closed flat on Friday ahead of the release of the revenue numbers. The benchmark index ended down 0.3%.

($1 = 32.8850 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: Malaysia's Manufacturing Sector Sales Grows 4.5% in November 2024
data.description:
The sales in the manufacturing sector in Malaysia grew 4.5% in November 2024 to 161.9 billion ringgit, faster than a 3% rise in the previous month, according to data released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia on Friday.

The growth was mainly driven by a double-digit jump of 12.3% in the food, beverages & tobacco sub-sector, faster than 11.2% in October, and a 7.1% rise in the electrical and electronics products sub-sector, quicker than 3.2% in October.

For the first eleven months of 2024, the sales value of the manufacturing sector rose 4.5% year on year to 1.728 trillion ringgit, the data showed.

data.title: Raging Los Angeles Fires Wreak Destruction — WSJ
data.description:
By Jennifer Calfas, Gareth Vipers and Meghan Bobrowsky

LOS ANGELES — Wildfires raged for the third straight day, destroying swaths of America's most populated county and displacing tens of thousands of residents, with firefighters struggling to contain the biggest blazes.

At least five infernos were burning simultaneously around Los Angeles, engulfing entire neighborhoods, destroying more than 10,000 structures and upending daily life in a historic disaster.

Five people were confirmed dead, but officials said that number was likely to increase in the coming days.

"The obvious question is, do you think it's going to grow?" Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna said at a media briefing Thursday evening. "I am praying it doesn't, but based on the devastation — it looks like an atomic bomb dropped in these areas — I'm not expecting good news."

Economic losses from the blazes have been estimated at tens of billions of dollars. Roughly 33,000 acres were aflame and more than 200,000 were without power across the wider Los Angeles area. More than 360,000 people are under mandatory evacuation orders, said Robert Fenton, Jr., a regional administrator at the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

The devastating wildfire in the affluent oceanside Pacific Palisades neighborhood has destroyed or damaged some 5,300 homes, businesses and other buildings. The nearly 20,000 acre blaze is one of the most destructive natural disasters in the city's history, Los Angeles Fire Department Chief Kristin Crowley said.

The Eaton fire in the Pasadena area, which damaged or destroyed more than 5,000 structures, was 13,600 acres Thursday.

And Thursday afternoon, a new fire in the West Hills neighborhood of L.A.'s San Fernando Valley ignited and spread into neighboring Ventura County. It quickly grew to nearly 1,000 acres and officials issued new evacuation orders and warnings, saying it was likely to grow rapidly because of wind conditions.

The blazes remained largely uncontained Thursday, stretching the region's firefighting resources to their limits. Nearly every corner of the sprawling Los Angeles area, home to some 10 million people, was smothered by a sky of smoke and ash. California National Guard units and firefighters from as far as Canada have been called to provide support.

Mayor Karen Bass pledged late Thursday that the city would look to clear red tape to aggressively rebuild affected areas. But officials cautioned that the scale of the devastation meant the process would be long and difficult.

"We know it's going to take a long time to get that debris" out, said Federal Emergency Management Agency Administrator Deanna Criswell. "We know that this recovery is going to be complicated. But it is doable."

President Biden said Thursday that the federal government will cover 100% of the disaster response for 180 days, up from the 75% it usually covers.

"I told the governor and local officials, spare no expense to do what they need to do and contain these fires," said Biden. The funds will cover expenses such as debris removal, temporary shelters and first responder salaries, he said.

The Los Angeles wildfires could be the costliest in U.S. history, with one JPMorgan analyst estimating total economic losses at close to $50 billion. The final tally of insurance losses from natural disasters can vary markedly from initial estimates.

Yalda Seace was staying at a hotel Thursday, still in disbelief of losing her home in Altadena, an area north of Pasadena. She watched via Ring camera as firefighters tried to put out the flames in her front yard on Tuesday night. Around 2 a.m., the Ring camera went dark.

When she returned to her three-bedroom house Wednesday morning, it was burned to the ground.

"It's like I'm in this horrible dream," Seace, an Orangetheory Fitness instructor and mother of three, said, adding that the Ring camera footage is seared into her memory. "I will never forget that."

Seace, her husband and their two Goldendoodles evacuated to her mother's home about 10 minutes away, bringing only a few clothes, shoes, and photos.

"I walked by things thinking, 'Oh, I'll take this,' and then I put it back thinking, 'No. I'm going to come back and see it tomorrow,' " she said.

She offered to bring a generator from her garage to her mom, whose home was still without power, before her sister reminded her that she didn't have a generator anymore.

"I don't have anything," she said. "It's not sunk in yet."

Firefighters stamped out a brush fire that broke out overnight in the Hollywood Hills near the Hollywood sign. The blaze forced further evacuations and caused the city fire chief to leave a press briefing. Conducting water drops over the 42.8-acre blaze from the air, firefighters fully contained the fire Thursday and residents were able to return to their homes.

Los Angeles officials intending to send an alert to people near the West Hills fire mistakenly issued a countywide evacuation warning Thursday afternoon, jolting residents who thought they were outside the danger zones. The mistake, caused by a technical error, went to more than 10 million residents, according to LA County Office of Emergency Management director Kevin McGowan. A second alert was issued to correct the error.

The blazes have been fanned by Santa Ana winds of up to 100 miles an hour — the speed of a Category 2 hurricane — and fueled by vegetation parched by a lack of rain in recent months.

Strong winds and dry conditions are expected to persist across Los Angeles and Ventura counties into Friday, with gusts up to 70 mph in some areas. Officials warned that high winds have been forecast to return early next week, potentially worsening any blazes that aren't brought under control over the weekend.

"There's a long way to go here before we see the conditions improving." said Brian Hurley, a meteorologist with the National Weather Service.

More than 7,500 personnel have been deployed to combat the blazes, including firefighters from neighboring states.

All schools in the Los Angeles Unified School District, the second largest in the country, were ordered closed through at least Friday because of fire danger and noxious smoke affecting much of the region. The mayor asked residents to stay off the roads unless they were evacuating.

The Palisades fire has left much of the neighborhood in ruins, including oceanside buildings long believed to be safe.

Actor Billy Crystal and his wife Janice said their Palisades home was completely destroyed by fire, while reality-TV star and hotel heiress Paris Hilton posted news footage to Instagram showing the smoldering ruins of her home.

Much of Hollywood, including studios operated by Disney, Warner Bros. Discovery and NBCUniversal, shut down production on their lots this week. Movie premieres were canceled and glitzy awards-season events were postponed.

An NBA game between the L.A. Lakers and the Charlotte Hornets at the Crypto.com Arena in downtown Los Angeles was postponed Thursday. The Los Angeles Rams said their NFL Wild Card playoff game against the Minnesota Vikings is still scheduled for Monday at SoFi Stadium, with the State Farm Stadium in Glendale, Ariz., as a backup location.

The fire threat has increased greatly in California and other parts of the West amid a hotter and drier climate and an expansion of homes into the wildland areas surrounding major cities. Sixteen of the 20 most destructive fires on record in California have broken out in the past decade, according to California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, or Cal Fire.

  • Sarah Krouse and Preetika Rana contributed to this article.

Write to Jennifer Calfas at jennifer.calfas@wsj.com, Gareth Vipers at gareth.vipers@wsj.com and Meghan Bobrowsky at meghan.bobrowsky@wsj.com

data.title: Luoniushan's Pig Sales Revenue Jumps 20% in December 2024; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Luoniushan's pig sales revenue jumped 20% year over year to 133.8 million yuan in December 2024, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Sales volume dropped 7.8% to 66,200 pigs during the month, the filing said.

For the whole year of 2024, sales revenue fell 2.3% to 1.31 billion yuan while sales volume declined 11% to 690,700 pigs, the filing said.

The company's shares dropped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Malaysia's Industrial Production Index Quickens to 3.6% in November 2024
data.description:
Malaysia's industrial production index rose 3.6% in November 2024, faster than a 2% rise in the previous month, according to data released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia on Friday.

The expansion was supported by the Manufacturing sector, which expanded by 4.6% versus 3.3% in the preceding month; a 3.9% rise in Electricity output versus a 1.9% growth in October 2024.

Moreover, the Mining sector production continued to decline, albeit marginally at 0.8% in November 2024 versus a 2.8% drop a month earlier.

Monthly, the index edged up 0.5% from 1.7% in October 2024, the data showed.
